headline: Models Split on Market Direction as Oil Fears Prove Unfounded
summary: Claude's bearish thesis based on $105 oil and inflation concerns completely backfired, with all five predictions wrong. Meanwhile, Grok and Perplexity correctly read the bullish momentum, particularly in tech stocks like NVDA which surged past all targets.

consensus_picks.note: Both models incorrectly predicted SPY decline due to oil/inflation fears, but the index rallied to $713.94 instead.
Both correctly called NVDA's rise, with the stock closing at $208.27, well above all predicted targets.
Despite earnings concerns, TSLA actually rose to $376.30, defying both models' bearish predictions.

best_call.summary: Grok nailed SPY's move to $713.94 with high confidence, earning the day's best score of 1.1.

worst_call.summary: Claude's high-confidence call for XLE to rise to $95.25 on oil surge was catastrophically wrong, with XLE actually plummeting to $56.87.
